sections.title: Test Coverage Analysis — To-Do List App
sections.content: **Current state: 0% coverage.** There's no `package.json`, no test runner, and no test files in the repo (only `app.js`, `index.html`, `styles.css`). Everything below is therefore a gap.

sections.title: 0. Blocker: the code isn't unit-testable as written
1. Untested functions (all 14 logic functions + 3 DOM-building functions)
sections.content:
| Function | Risk if broken |
|---|---|
| `loadTodos` | corrupts/loses persisted data |
| `saveTodos` | silent data loss |
| `createId` | duplicate IDs → wrong item edited/deleted |
| `addTodo` | blank/whitespace todos slip through |
| `updateTodo` / `toggleTodo` / `deleteTodo` | wrong todo mutated |
| `clearCompleted` / `toggleAll` | bulk-action bugs |
| `filteredTodos` | wrong items shown per filter |
| `getActiveCount` / `getCompletedCount` | wrong counter, breaks pluralization |
| `render` / `createTodoItem` | UI desync, XSS if ever switched to `innerHTML` |
| `startEdit` | double-save/double-cancel race |

sections.title: 2. Edge cases not covered
sections.content: - `loadTodos`: `localStorage.getItem` call sits **outside** the try/catch — if it throws (Safari private mode, storage disabled), it's unhandled. - `loadTodos`: valid JSON that isn't an array (`{}`, `"x"`, `42`) is silently ignored — `state.todos` stays whatever it was, no warning. - `addTodo("")`, `addTodo(" ")` → no-op, must verify `saveTodos`/`render` are **not** called. - `toggleTodo`/`updateTodo` with a nonexistent id → silent no-op, `sync()` not called. - `deleteTodo` with a nonexistent id → array unchanged but `sync()` **is still called** (asymmetric vs. `toggleTodo`) — re-saves/re-renders for nothing. - `clearCompleted` when nothing is completed → early return, no save. - `toggleAll` on empty list → no-op; on all-completed list → un-completes everything (toggle-back). - `filteredTodos` with an unrecognized filter string → falls through to "all" silently. - `render` pluralization boundaries: 0 / 1 / 2 active todos (PT-BR singular/plural text). - `render` empty state has two different messages (no todos at all vs. no todos matching filter). - `window.addEventListener("storage", ...)`: a real `storage.clear()` fires a `StorageEvent` with `key === null`, which the `event.key === STORAGE_KEY` check **misses** — cross-tab clear won't refresh the UI. - `startEdit`: Enter then blur firing back-to-back must not double-save (the `finished` flag guards this — untested). - `startEdit`: saving an edit to an empty/whitespace string must discard the edit, not delete or blank the todo. - XSS regression: todo text containing `` must render as literal text (current code uses `textContent`, good — but unguarded by a test).

sections.title: 3 & 4. Missing error-handling tests
sections.content:
- `saveTodos` has **no try/catch** — `localStorage.setItem` quota errors (large lists, private mode) throw uncaught and abort `sync()`, leaving in-memory state changed but UI not re-rendered.
- `loadTodos`'s `getItem` call is unguarded (see above).
- No defensive handling if `addTodo`/`updateTodo` ever receive non-string/`null` input (only called internally today with safe values, but worth locking the contract with a test).

sections.title: 5. Integration test gaps (full DOM flow, nothing currently exercises these)
sections.content:
Add → render → counters; toggle via checkbox click; delete via trash click; filter button switching + `aria`/active class; clear-completed button enable/disable + click; mark-all/unmark-all label switching; full inline-edit flow (dblclick → input → Enter/Escape/blur); persistence across reload; cross-tab `storage` event sync; keyboard interactions (Enter to add, Escape to clear input).

---

sections.title: Test skeletons

rawOutputPreview:
## Test Coverage Analysis — To-Do List App

**Current state: 0% coverage.** There's no `package.json`, no test runner, and no test files in the repo (only `app.js`, `index.html`, `styles.css`). Everything below is therefore a gap.

### 0. Blocker: the code isn't unit-testable as written

`app.js` declares all logic as top-level `function` statements in a plain script (no `module.exports`, no ES `export`). If you `require('./app.js')` from a test, Node wraps the file in a function scope — `addTodo`, `toggleTodo`, `filteredTodos`, etc. never become reachable from outside. The script also runs its setup (`loadTodos(); render();`) immediately on load and queries `document.getElementById(...)` at the top, so importing it requires a matching DOM already in place.

Recommended minimal fix (no behavior change, browser-safe):
```js
// end of app.js
if (typeof module !== "undefined" && module.exports) {
  module.exports = {
    state, loadTodos, saveTodos, createId, addTodo, updateTodo, toggleTodo,
    deleteTodo, clearCompleted, toggleAll, setFilter, filteredTodos,
    getActiveCount, getCompletedCount,
  };
}
```
Until that lands, true unit tests aren't possible — only DOM-driven integration tests are. I've written skeletons for both tracks below; say the word if you want me to apply the export shim and a `package.json` with Jest.

---

### 1. Untested functions (all 14 logic functions + 3 DOM-building functions)

| Function | Risk if broken |
|---|---|
| `loadTodos` | corrupts/loses persisted data |
| `saveTodos` | silent data loss |
| `createId` | duplicate IDs → wrong item edited/deleted |
| `addTodo` | blank/whitespace todos slip through |
| `updateTodo` / `toggleTodo` / `deleteTodo` | wrong todo mutated |
| `clearCompleted` / `toggleAll` | bulk-action bugs |
| `filteredTodos` | wrong items shown per filter |
| `getActiveCount` / `getCompletedCount` | wrong counter, breaks pluralization |
| `render` / `createTodoItem` | UI desync, XSS if ever switched to `innerHTML` |
|
